steps: Divide \(x^2 - 2x - 5\) by \(x - 3\) using polynomial long division. First step: \(x^2 \div x = x\); multiply: \(x(x-3)=x^2-3x\); subtract: \((x^2-2x-5)-(x^2-3x)=x-5\).
Second step: \(x \div x = 1\); multiply: \(1(x-3)=x-3\); subtract: \((x-5)-(x-3)=-2\). Quotient is \(x+1\) with remainder \(-2\), so \(\frac{x^2-2x-5}{x-3}=x+1-\frac{2}{x-3}\).
Verify by checking the remainder: if \(x=4\), LHS \(=\frac{16-8-5}{1}=3\); RHS \(=4+1-\frac{2}{1}=3\). Confirmed.

Evaluate all four choices:
  • A: \(x-5-\frac{20}{x-3}\) — quotient \(x-5\) would require a different long division step; remainder 20 does not match \(-2\). Eliminated.
  • B: \(x-5-\frac{10}{x-3}\) — same incorrect quotient \(x-5\); remainder 10 does not match. Eliminated.
  • C: \(x+1-\frac{8}{x-3}\) — quotient \(x+1\) is correct but remainder should be \(-2\), not \(-8\). Eliminated.
  • D: \(x+1-\frac{2}{x-3}\) — matches quotient \(x+1\) and remainder \(-2\) exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
